items.title: Fears $1m to remove lead-contaminated soil in Captains Flat 'won't go very far'

items.title: Austria-Algeria draw sparks World Cup match fixing allegations online
items.description: Austria and Algeria's dramatic 3-3 World Cup draw sparked online claims the match was fixed to send both teams into the knockout stage at Iran's expense. Conspiracy theories, misleading videos and fake FIFA notices have fuelled the speculation, with some dubbing it the "Disgrace of Kansas City" in a nod to the infamous 1982 "Disgrace of Gijón". But is there any evidence the game was engineered?

items.title: Former ethics lawyer says Trump's crypto poses 'clear conflict of interest'
items.description: Former White House ethics lawyer Richard Painter says President Trump "stands alone" in having substantial financial conflicts of interest and that, "for every other executive branch official, it would be a violation."

items.title: Algeria votes for new parliament with turnout and cost-of-living in focus

FRANCE 24's Claire Paccalin tells us more about how the elections works, and in what context they're taking place.

items.title: Gazans take stock of the war 1,000 days after its start
items.description: 1,000 days since the massacres of October 7th means 1,000 since total war was unleashed on the more than 2 million residents of the Gaza strip. Israeli retaliation to the Hamas massacre is said to have killed more than 73,000 people and wounded many more, including since the ceasefire of October 2025. Benjamin Netanyahu hopes to expand the security zone further and to cover 70% of Gaza, despite a UN warning that this is intensifying risks to civilians and further constraining humanitarian efforts.

items.title: Andy Burnham outlines his vision for UK economy with promise to raise living standards
items.description: Andy Burnham, the former mayor of Greater Manchester, is likely to be the next British prime minister following Keir Starmer's resignation. Central to Burnham's platform is improving living standards that have stagnated or even fallen since the 2008 global financial crisis. But with weak public finances, how realistic is his plan? FRANCE 24's Charles Pellegrin talks to Renaud Foucart, senior lecturer in economics at Lancaster University.

items.title: Schismatic Catholic society sees six bishops excommunicated by Pope
items.description: The Vatican has excommunicated six bishops from the ultraconservative Society of St Pius X, while warning that any lay believers who "formally adhere" to the breakaway Catholic group would suffer the same fate. It comes a day after the traditionalist group consecrated four new bishops, openly defying a plea from Pope Leo XIV to desist and exacerbating a rift dating from 1988.

items.title: Unprecedented images show how Russia is using drones from the war in Ukraine in Africa
items.description: Russian mercenaries from the Africa Corps, which has replaced the well-known Wagner Group in providing support to the Malian army, are deploying new combat drones in Mali. Frequently found on the frontlines in Ukraine, these drones are now entering the Sahel region – an environment for which they were not designed.

items.title: Ukrainian charged in Germany over Nord Stream blasts
items.description: Ukraine denies involvement in the case which may have serious implications for its relationship with Germany.

items.title: South African police say anti-immigrant protests were 'mostly peaceful'
items.description: South African police said Wednesday that nationwide anti-immigration protests held the previous day were "mostly peaceful", despite isolated incidents of looting and criminal activity.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Spain cruise into last 16 with win over Austria
items.description: Spain booked their place in the World Cup last 16 with an emphatic 3-0 win over Austria on Thursday, producing another polished performance that rarely gave their opponents a foothold.

items.title: Egypt to give Salah until last minute to prove fitness for Socceroos clash
items.description: The "passionate" Egypt captain trains with the squad but his coach says he won't be rushed into the team to face Australia if he's not fit.

items.title: German coalition agrees on sweeping reform package
items.description: After its first year in office, Germany's coalition government is seeking to revive a sluggish economy and push through key changes with its broad package of reforms. The plans met with a mixed reaction.

items.title: India news: Narendra Modi hosts Japan's Sanae Takaichi
items.description: Sanae Takaichi is on her first visit to India since becoming PM, and has held talks with Narendra Modi to boost bilateral ties.

items.title: Trips, wealth and property – The judicial cases closing in on Manuel Adorni
items.description: Investigators have ordered a forensic review of the former Cabinet chief's finances and are examining the circumstances of his private jet trip to Uruguay and real-estate investments. Leer más

items.description: Around the world, childhood is being reprogrammed by digital technologies that shape how young people learn, play and connect. Leer más

items.title: Explosion in Monaco: Who is Vadym Yermolaiev?
items.description: Ukrainian-born businessman Vadym Yermolaiev was reportedly injured in a suspected assassination attempt in Monaco. Kyiv had previously imposed sanctions on him. What is known about the case?

items.title: How haircuts are helping men recover at a Kenyan mental health hospital
items.description: At Kenya’s largest mental health referral hospital, male patients are offered a different kind of therapy: a personal grooming session provided by two visiting barbers. Healthcare workers say personal grooming is an important aspect of recovery, as patients often lose interest in self-care.

items.title: Firm linked to Otudeko’s Honeywell Group buys 14% stake in Ikeja Hotel
items.description: Using Thursday’s closing share price, the firm’s holding in Ikeja Hotel is worth more than N13.5 billion. The post Firm linked to Otudeko’s Honeywell Group buys 14% stake in Ikeja Hotel appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Nissan management shake-up plans reported as sharks circle
items.description: Nissan is considering a change in leadership following poor results and the failure of a proposed merger with Honda, according to a report citing unnamed sources.

items.title: Reports of Adorni's imminent departure from government ramp up
items.description: Rumours circulating that Cabinet Chief Manuel Adorni will leave the government imminently as further lurid headlines emerge in the press. Leer más

items.title: Argentina continue knockout preparations in scorching Kansas City heat
items.description: Lionel Scaloni's side returned to training under an extreme heat warning with three selection questions still to resolve ahead of Friday's last-16 clash against Cape Verde. Leer más

items.title: Explosion in feral pig numbers attributed to major flooding in far north SA
items.description: Feral pig numbers are exploding in outback South Australia after record-breaking floods, prompting a call from station owners and the Invasive Species Council for governments to take action.

items.title: ‘My dream is broken’: Japan visa rules push out foreign residents
items.description: Japan’s new visa rules on foreign nationals pushes out exsisting immigrants

items.title: New UN report links Joseph Kabila more closely to AFC/M23
items.description: A new United Nations Group of Experts report paints the clearest picture yet of the AFC/M23’s military strength and political ambitions in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo, while highlighting the movement’s increasingly close ties with former President Joseph Kabila.

items.title: ‘Miracle’: Trapped man rescued eight days after Venezuela earthquakes
items.description: Attention has turned to the humanitarian response after at least 2,295 people are confirmed dead, and 13,000 homeless.

items.title: Sri Lanka upgraded to upper-middle income economy by World Bank
items.description: Sri Lanka first entered the upper-middle-income category in 2019 before falling back to lower-middle-income status as economic growth slowed and income levels deteriorated

items.title: Thailand: Child crashes vehicle into group of monks
items.description: An 11-year-old boy crashed a pickup truck belonging to his parents into a group of monks on a pilgrimage, police said. Eight were killed in the incident and 14 more were hospitalized in Mukdahan province.

items.title: Live: ASX set to rise after Dow record
items.description: The ASX is expected to open higher after weaker US jobs data eased interest rate hike expectations. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: Ramaphosa heads to Kinshasa as Africa rallies behind DRC’s Ebola fight
items.description: South African President Cyril Ramaphosa arrived in Kinshasa on Thursday for a high-level African Union solidarity mission aimed at reinforcing continental support for the Democratic Republic of the Congo's response to its latest Ebola outbreak.

items.title: From friendship bracelets to wedding rings: A Taylor and Travis timeline
items.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are expected to celebrate their wedding with a party at New York's Madison Square Garden this weekend.

items.description: The explosion near the main courthouse complex left 20 others wounded

items.title: Amid the World Cup, the new pan-Africanism is conditional
items.description: A record number of African nations are at the 2026 World Cup, but the continent no longer backs them all.

items.description: President hopes trusted former PRO operator can help La Libertad Avanza regain the initiative after Manuel Adorni's resignation and push the scandal off the agenda. Leer más

items.title: Did this AI anti-drug video make drugs look appealing?
items.description: Hong Kong’s Correctional Services Department removed an AI-generated K-pop video, after facing backlash that it made substances look too appealing.

items.title: Why are South Korean fans so angry at their football coach?
items.description: Hong Myung-bo has resigned after South Korea failed to advance to the World Cup knockout stages.

items.title: DRC and Senegal crash out of World Cup after losing early leads
items.description: A disappointing end for Senegal and the Democratic Republic of the Congo, who both had their opponents on the ropes before seeing their World Cup dreams evaporate.

items.description: Israel has rejected a UN Commission report that says its army deliberately targets Palestinian children.

items.title: Argentina’s beef export boom to United States leaves locals paying more for steak
items.description: Argentina’s exports to the United States are expanding fast past premium steaks to lower-quality cuts. That's great for exporters, but worse for locals, who see prices becoming less affordable. Leer más

items.title: Vatican excommunicates followers of global Catholic sect
items.description: Around 600,000 followers of the Society of Saint Pius X, a Catholic sect, are affected.

items.title: Canadian boy, 11, dies of rabies after waking to bat on his face
items.description: Rabies infections are rare in Canada. There have been 28 human deaths due to rabies in the country since 1924.

items.title: Six tourists killed, three others missing after boat capsizes in Pakistan's Swat Valley
items.description: The incident took place at Saifullah Lake in the Kalam Valley of Upper Swat district, a popular tourist destination

items.title: Will alleged crime ties damage Serbia's ruling party?
items.description: Pressure on President Aleksandar Vucic is growing as years of investigations into alleged links between the state and organized crime coincide with a gang war. Experts say the issue is eroding support within his base.

items.title: Fact check: Racist German fan petition story false
items.description: It has been claimed that following Germany's World Cup exit, some fans launched a petition seeking to ban "African" and "Muslim" players from the national team — and that DW reported the story. This is not true.

items.title: Gold smuggling from DRC's Ituri province to Uganda on the rise, says UN
items.description: Gold smuggling from DR Congo to Uganda is on the rise, according to a new UN report. Experts assessing the situation in the DRC for sanctions review pointed to discrepancies between Uganda’s stated gold production and its export levels in 2025.

items.title: How Rotary’s values, 4-Way Test can lift up Nigeria, By Ehi Braimah
items.description: A better Nigeria begins with better values. Rotary has shown that when service, integrity and ethical leadership become a way of life, communities prosper. There is every reason to believe that the same principles can help lift up Nigeria. The task of nation-building, as I have written in previous articles, belongs to all Nigerians – […] The post How Rotary’s values, 4-Way Test can lift up Nigeria, By Ehi Braimah appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Ceuzany, Cape Verde's golden voice with volcanic emotion
items.description: Barefoot, arms raised and teeming with emotion, Ceuzany belts out the signature track from her latest album, a song dedicated to the fight against domestic violence, in a powerful voice that sends shivers through the Cape Verde bar

items.title: Zimbabwean citizen recounts leaving South Africa over anti-migrant sentiment
items.description: Around 25,000 migrants have left South Africa in recent weeks over rising anti-migrant sentiment that has resulted in the deaths of four people. One returnee from Zimbabwe recounts his experience.

items.title: EU slaps €3 duty on low-value imported packages
items.description: The EU has moved to curb what it sees as unfair competition from mostly Chinese online retailers by imposing its levy. It follows a similar move from the US.

items.title: Ivory Coast fans disappointed after 2-1 Norway knockout
items.description: Fans of the Ivory Coast soccer team gathered on Tuesday in Abidjan, the capital of the Ivory Coast, to watch their team play against Haaland’s Norway. But a slight tap from Haaland’s left foot in the 86th minute was the difference as Norway beat Ivory Coast 2-1 on Tuesday.

items.title: U.S. service member missing after Seahawk helicopter goes down in Arabian Sea
items.description: No ‌indication ⁠the crash was caused ⁠by hostile action, U.S. military said

items.title: NATO plans to replace AWACS with Saab GlobalEye jets, sources say
items.description: NATO dropped plans in 2025 to buy six Boeing E-7 Wedgetail aircraft after the Pentagon, its largest prospective customer, scrapped plans to acquire 26 of the jets ‌and instead put greater emphasis on satellite-based capabilities

items.title: Live: PM says gambling ad reforms go further than Murphy report 'in some ways'
items.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says the government's gambling advertising reforms go further than Peta Murphy's report. Follow live.

items.title: Nine Thai monks killed after 11-year-old driver collides with procession
items.description: The group of 35 monks and five lay followers were walking by the roadside when the truck ploughed into them.

items.title: Residents of Ethiopian town forced to kill hundreds of their own dogs after rabies deaths
items.description: Some residents reluctantly hung their dogs to avoid being arrested or fined, locals tell the BBC.
